codeReview.summary: The feature adds useful malformed-JSON handling and broader tests, but it does not fully satisfy the MCP error-handling requirements. Two blocking gaps remain: fleet_status still drops actionable SSH failure details, and invalid JSON-RPC messages that are valid JSON can be silently ignored instead of receiving the required protocol error response.

issues.description: fleet_status still discards the underlying SSH failure details during the connectivity probe and collapses every connection failure into `error: "Host unreachable"` (see lines 132-145). That does not meet VAL-MCP-010's requirement for actionable/descriptive error content, because callers lose the concrete stderr/exit-code context needed to diagnose why the host failed.

The new `server.server.onerror` handler only emits a JSON-RPC response when it heuristically classifies the transport error as a parse error (lines 96-112). Valid JSON but invalid JSON-RPC payloads are still unhandled: during review, `printf '[]\n' | timeout 5 node apps/mcp-server/dist/index.js` and `printf '{"foo":"bar"}\n' | timeout 5 node apps/mcp-server/dist/index.js` both produced no protocol response. VAL-MCP-011 requires graceful handling of invalid JSON-RPC with appropriate error codes, so malformed requests are still not fully covered.

The new test named `error content includes enough detail to be actionable` only checks that each host has a truthy `error` field plus `name`/`hostname` (lines 312-353); it never verifies that the actual SSH failure details are surfaced. The suite also omits any stdio case for valid JSON / invalid JSON-RPC requests, which is why the remaining VAL-MCP-011 gap went unnoticed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mcp-worker skill does not explicitly require malformed-request verification beyond basic stdio initialization/tools-list checks, so workers are not prompted to probe valid JSON but invalid JSON-RPC payloads even though VAL-MCP-011 is broader than plain parse errors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mcp-worker/SKILL.md:90-104 only prescribes initialize/tools-list manual verification; validation-contract.md:301-303 requires graceful handling of invalid JSON-RPC with appropriate error codes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library lacks an MCP-specific note explaining that the SDK's stdio transport surfaces decode/validation failures via `onerror` without automatically sending a JSON-RPC error response, even though the implementation now depends on that behavior and future workers will need to reason about it carefully.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/mcp-server/src/index.ts:96-112 contains a custom stdout workaround for transport errors; .factory/library only contains architecture.md, environment.md, and user-testing.md, and grep there found only general MCP references rather than transport/error-handling guidance.

summary: Review failed. The commit correctly adds -32700 responses for malformed JSON and expands test coverage, but it still misses part of the stated error-handling contract: fleet_status errors are not actionable enough, and invalid JSON-RPC messages that are syntactically valid JSON can still be dropped without any JSON-RPC error response. Report written after reviewing the handoff, transcript skeleton, diff, changed code, tests, skill file, mission docs, services file, and shared library.
